hero.headline: Best Beatbot Sora Robotic Pool Cleaner for 2026
hero.ratingText: Sora 70 vs Sora 30 vs Sora 10: which one should you buy?
hero.description: This guide helps you choose the right Beatbot Sora model based on pool size, cleaning coverage, surface debris pickup, runtime, debris capacity, and overall value.
hero.imageAlt: Beatbot Sora 70 cleaning debris on a backyard pool surface
hero.imageClass: rounded-lg shadow-lg w-full h-[460px] object-cover object-center

productMain.heading: Beatbot Sora 70 Overview
bullets: Best overall pick for full pool, surface, wall, waterline, floor, and shallow-area cleaning
6800 GPH suction power for leaves, pollen, insects, sand, and everyday debris
Up to 5 hours of floor cleaning with coverage up to about 3,200 sq. ft.
Smart surface parking and one-touch app retrieval

items.title: Full-Pool Coverage
items.description: The Sora 70 is built to handle surface debris, pool floors, walls, waterline grime, and shallow platform zones.

items.imageAlt: Beatbot robotic pool cleaner debris basket
items.title: Large Debris Capacity
items.description: A 6L basket on Sora 70 and 5L-plus capacity on other Sora models help reduce interruptions during routine cleaning.

items.title: Smart Easy Retrieval
items.description: Smart surface parking and water release make pickup easier after a cleaning cycle, especially in deeper pools.

storage.label: QUICK WINNERS SUMMARY
storage.heading: Best Picks At A Glance
storage.text: Sora 70 is the premium pick for the most complete coverage. Sora 30 is the best value for most pools. Sora 10 is the simplest entry choice for cordless robotic cleaning.
bullets: Best For Large Pools: Sora series coverage up to about 3,229 sq. ft.

winners.title: Best Overall: Sora 70
winners.bestFor: Best for large pools and surface debris
winners.specs: Coverage: up to about 3,200 sq. ft. | Runtime: up to 5 hours floor mode | Capacity: 6L
winners.description: Choose this if you want the most complete Sora model, including water surface cleaning and premium full-pool coverage.

winners.title: Best Value: Sora 30
winners.specs: Coverage: up to about 3,229 sq. ft. | Runtime: up to 300 minutes | Capacity: 5L
winners.description: The better fit for most pool owners who want strong suction, smart parking, and broad cleaning coverage without buying the flagship model.

winners.title: Best Entry Pick: Sora 10
winners.specs: Coverage: up to about 3,229 sq. ft. | Runtime: up to 300 minutes | Capacity: 5L
winners.description: A simpler cordless option for everyday pool cleaning, with floor, wall, waterline, and shallow-area support.

performance.label: REAL WORLD CLEANING
performance.heading: Built For Leaves, Sand, Pollen, And Waterline Grime
performance.text: The Sora lineup focuses on high suction, large debris baskets, smart parking, and pool-surface coverage that helps reduce manual brushing, netting, and vacuuming during swim season.
performance.imageAlt: Beatbot Sora 70 removing leaves from a pool surface

items.title: Choose Sora 70 if...
items.text: You want the most complete cleaning coverage, including water surface debris pickup, premium full-pool coverage, and the largest debris capacity in this lineup.

items.title: Choose Sora 30 if...
items.text: You want strong robotic cleaning performance at a more balanced price, with broad coverage, smart parking, and enough capacity for most family pools.

items.title: Choose Sora 10 if...
items.text: You want a simpler cordless cleaner for routine maintenance and do not need every premium feature offered by the Sora 70.

usage.imageAlt: Beatbot Sora 30 climbing a pool wall
usage.label: WEEKLY POOL CARE
usage.heading: A Cleaner Pool With Less Manual Work
usage.text: For pool owners who want fewer manual touch-ups, a cordless robotic cleaner can simplify weekly maintenance by handling routine floor debris, walls, and waterline buildup automatically.

Cordless design avoids hose and cable management
Smart surface or waterline parking makes retrieval easier

Options for different budgets and cleaning needs

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Which Sora model is best for leaves?
items.answer: Sora 70 is the strongest choice for leaves because it combines high suction, a larger 6L debris basket, and water surface cleaning for floating debris.

items.question: Do I need surface cleaning or is floor and wall cleaning enough?
items.answer: If your pool collects floating leaves, pollen, or insects, surface cleaning is useful. If most debris sinks to the bottom, Sora 30 or Sora 10 may be enough.

items.question: Is Sora 70 worth it over Sora 30?
items.answer: Sora 70 is worth considering if you want the most complete coverage and surface debris pickup. Sora 30 is the better value if you mainly need floor, wall, and waterline cleaning.

items.question: Which model should I choose for a large pool?
items.answer: For larger or debris-heavy pools, start with Sora 70. For average family pools where value matters more than surface cleaning, Sora 30 is the more balanced pick.

faq.bannerImageAlt: Beatbot robotic pool cleaner in a bright pool scene

finalVerdict.heading: Final Verdict
finalVerdict.text: The Beatbot Sora 70 is the strongest overall recommendation for pool owners who want the most complete cleaning coverage. The Sora 30 is the smarter value pick, while the Sora 10 is the easiest entry point into the lineup.

finalCta.heading: Find the Right Beatbot Sora for Your Pool
finalCta.description: Start with Sora 70 for the most complete cleaning, or compare the value and entry picks before you buy.
